title: oxalis seatonii
intro: Oxalis seatonii, also known as the Red Sorrel or Seaton's Wood-sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its high content of oxalic acid, which has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties. Oxalis seatonii has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, arthritis, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Oxalis seatonii include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its health benefits.
